title: 10 best herbal essential oils for gum infection
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for gum infection are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate the symptoms of gum infection, also known as periodontitis, by reducing inflammation and fighting bacteria. The best herbal essential oils for gum infection include Eucalyptus globulus, Melaleuca alternifolia, Syzygium aromaticum, Cinnamomum zeylanicum, Thymus vulgaris, Cymbopogon citratus, Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, Lavandula angustifolia, and Mentha x piperita. If left untreated, gum infection can lead to tooth loss, painful chewing, and difficulty eating, significantly impacting one's quality of life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat the bacteria that cause gum disease. The oil's main compound, terpinen-4-ol, has been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus mutans and other pathogens responsible for periodontal infections. Additionally, tea tree oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections, promoting a healthy mouth environment. By incorporating tea tree essential oils into your oral care routine, you can naturally soothe and treat gum inflammation.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Help to combat bacteria and reduce inflammation in gum infections by killing off the pathogens that cause them.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Antioxidant properties: Protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum disease progression.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antimicrobial compounds, tea tree leaves help combat bacterial and fungal infections that cause gum inflammation.
Buds: Containing high levels of cineole, a potent anti-inflammatory agent, tea tree buds aid in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia plants in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves using a clean cloth to remove dirt and moisture.
Steam distill 100g of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves in a 500ml capacity still with 200ml water for 60 minutes.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled liquid through decantation and filter it using a coffee filter paper.
Store the resulting melaleuca alternifolia essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight lids at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which allow it to effectively combat the bacteria that cause gingivitis and periodontal disease. The oil's compounds, such as eucalyptol and alpha-pinene, have been shown to reduce inflammation and kill off pathogenic microorganisms, promoting a healthy balance in the mouth. Additionally, Tasmanian blue gum essential oils' decongestant properties help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with gum infection, making it an effective natural remedy for oral health issues.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil has been shown to exhibit strong antibacterial activity against various bacteria, including those responsible for gum infections, such as Streptococcus and Staphylococcus species. This property helps to combat the infection-causing pathogens.
Anti-inflammatory property: The oil's anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections, promoting a more rapid recovery and minimizing tissue damage.

Antioxidant property: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil is rich in antioxidants, which help protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by free radicals, thereby supporting the body's natural defense mechanisms and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and fight against bacterial infections, making them effective in treating gum infection.
Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contain tannins and phenolic acids, which have antimicrobial properties and help to combat oral pathogens responsible for gum infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of eucalyptus globulus in the early morning dew.
Cleanse the harvested leaves by rinsing them gently with distilled water and air drying.
Steam distill the cleaned leaves using a still at 100°c for 4-6 hours to extract essential oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil through activated charcoal and store it in dark glass bottles at room temperature.
Dilute the extracted essential oil with carrier oils like coconut or olive oil in a ratio of 1:3 for topical application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pigeon pea essential oils helps with gum infection because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains a compound called cucurbitacin, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause periodontal disease. Additionally, pigeon pea essential oil's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation in the gums, promoting healing and reducing pain. By applying topical applications of this oil, individuals can effectively combat gum infections and prevent further damage to their oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: The seeds contain compounds that exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi, which could help combat bacterial infections that contribute to gum disease.
Anti-inflammatory: Pigeon Pea seeds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
Antioxidant: The seeds are rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, pigeon pea roots help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
Seeds: Containing antimicrobial agents, pigeon pea seeds inhibit bacterial growth and promote healing in infected gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh cajanus cajan leaves and chop them finely into small pieces.
Combine the chopped leaves with 500 milliliters of carrier oil in a dark glass bottle and let it steep for 48 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container and discard the solids.
Transfer the extracted oil to a clean, sterilized glass bottle and add 10 drops of grapefruit seed extract as a natural preservative.
Store the finished essential oil in a cool, dark place for up to six months and use it twice daily as a mouthwash.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram essential oils helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat bacterial growth and reduce inflammation. The oil's active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to inhibit the development of biofilm-forming bacteria that contribute to gum infections. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh origanum vulgare leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Wash the harvested leaves with cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Steam distill the washed leaves using 10 grams of leaves per 100 milliliters of distilled water for 4 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper into dark glass bottles.
Store the origanum vulgare essential oil in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight.

remedy_recipe: Harvest thymus vulgaris leaves fresh in the morning when they are dewy to ensure optimal oil content.
Wash the harvested thymus vulgaris leaves thoroughly with distilled water and gentle soap.
Steam distill the washed thymus vulgaris leaves using a clevenger apparatus for 2-3 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers in abundance.
Dry the plant material thoroughly to prevent any mold growth during processing.
Crush the dried plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area.
Steam distill the crushed plant material using a still and collect the essential oil.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia leaves and flowers at dawn or dusk when oil content is highest.
Dry the harvested materials in a warm, dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Use a high-quality still or cold press to extract the essential oil from dried plant material within 30 minutes.
Distill 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia with 500ml of distilled water at a temperature range of 65-75°c for 1 hour.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or paper towel to remove any impurities and sediment within 15 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested flowers using a dehydrator or air-drying method for 24 hours.
Steam distill 100 grams of dried calendula flowers with 500 milliliters of water at atmospheric pressure for 2 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter and store it in a dark glass bottle.
Allow the essential oil to mature for 24 hours before using it topically for gum infection treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with gum infection because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 pounds of fresh ginger root from your local garden or market.
Dry the ginger in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 6 hours.
Grind the dried ginger into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar.
Steep 100 grams of the powdered ginger in 1 liter of high-proof vodka for 14 days.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 4-6 hours or air drying for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried foeniculum vulgare plant material with water in a still at atmospheric pressure for 2-3 hours.
Filter the obtained essential oil through a coffee filter paper into dark glass bottles and store them in a cool place.
Mix 20 drops of foeniculum vulgare essential oil with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil and apply it to the affected gum area twice daily.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gum infection with herbal essential oils is to dilute them with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Tea tree oil, known for its antimicrobial properties, can help combat bacteria and reduce inflammation. Apply the blend to the affected area using a cotton swab three times a day. supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum infection that are treated with herbal essential oils are tooth decay, mouth ulcers, and bad breath. remedy_recipe: Harvest mature cinnamomum zeylanicum branches with at least 50% of their leaves intact for efficient oil production.
Steam distill the harvested branches in a ratio of 1 part leaves to 2 parts branches for 2 hours.
Collect the condensed steam that contains the essential oil and transfer it to a glass container for further processing.
Allow the essential oil to settle and separate from any impurities for 2-3 hours before decanting it into another container.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its quality and shelf life.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 pound of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers dried at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material at 100 degrees celsius for 2 hours to yield 0.5 milliliters per kilogram.
Collect the resulting distillate and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter paper to remove any remaining impurities and debris.
Store the obtained thymus vulgaris essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid at 4 degrees celsius.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of cymbopogon citratus by cutting them in the early morning to prevent oil degradation.
Steam distill 10 kilograms of the harvested leaves in 10 liters of water at 100°c for 3 hours.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from the aqueous phase using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter to remove any impurities and contaminants.
Store the cymbopogon citratus essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale roots in the early morning when the dew is still present on the surface.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm well-ventilated area for 7 to 10 days with temperature not exceeding 40 degrees celsius.
Steam distill the dried roots using a steam distillation apparatus at a temperature of 100 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Collect the essential oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel and then filter the oil through a cotton cloth.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with gum infection because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause gum infections. These properties also reduce inflammation and promote healing, making it an effective natural remedy for gum infections and promoting overall oral health. This helps to prevent further damage and promote a healthy gum environment.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as an antibacterial agent, helping to combat the bacterial growth responsible for gum infections.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
Caryophyllene oxide: Possesses antimicrobial properties, helping to combat the bacterial overgrowth that causes gum infections and promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used to make essential oils because they are rich in antimicrobial properties that help combat gum infections.
Flowers: Flowers are used to make essential oils because they have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help reduce swelling and prevent infection in gums.
Stems: Stems are used to make essential oils because they contain compounds that have antibacterial properties, which can help treat gum infections by preventing the growth of bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers on a warm sunny day when they are in full bloom.
Dry the fresh flowers in a single layer using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 24 hours.
Steam distill 200g of the dried flowers in a distillation apparatus using 2kg of distilled water for 2 hours.
Collect the essential oil produced during steam distillation and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the final product in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid and keep it in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils helps with gum infection because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's high concentration of menthol and menthone gives it strong antibacterial effects, which can help combat the bacteria that cause gum infections. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, promoting healing and soothing the gums. This natural remedy can provide relief from gum inflammation and infection, promoting overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: It helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain in the gums by acting as a local anesthetic and anti-inflammatory agent.
Menthone: It possesses antimicrobial properties that can help combat the bacterial causes of gum infections, thereby promoting healing and reducing the risk of infection spread.
Caryophyllene oxide: It acts as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, which helps to reduce swelling, prevent tissue damage, and promote overall healing in the affected gum area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in menthol and menthone, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties to help soothe gum infections.
Stems: Contain a high concentration of menthol, which helps reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with gum infections.
Roots: Have a higher concentration of menthone and other compounds that help combat bacterial infections and reduce inflammation in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the early morning after dew has evaporated.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at a temperature of 100-110°c for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distilled steam and condense it into a separate container within 1 hour.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water through decantation and filtration within 30 minutes.
Store the obtained essential oil in a dark glass bottle and keep it away from direct sunlight.
